sprint: Repository-Only Cards — real read-only View + deep Clone
task: Direct follow-up: 'why can't it be a deep clone?' and 'by view only means u cant edit them but u can still open them in read only mode.' A Repository-only card now opens a real Workspace (Official, read-only) or clones into a real editable World (Personal with no backup), instead of a plain info modal. New shared _materializeProjectFromPackage() un-flattens a compiled Theme's layouts/frameVariations/representations/layerPack straight back into real, individually editable per-item files -- lossless for these -- while disclosing the one hard limit: Scenes/Places/Experiences are a confirmed one-way compile with no reverse path, so a materialized World with no Scenes opens showing the honest 'No Scene yet' state, not a browsable reconstruction; that data still round-trips through Validate/Build/Publish regardless. Official opens via the existing View Mode machinery (never persisted, relying on the pre-existing _persist() read-only backstop); leaving View Mode needed zero new UI, since the Workspace's own overflow-menu Duplicate World already deep-copies whatever Project is currently open with no assumption it was ever itself saved. Verified via Playwright: zero ProjectStore writes before any action in View, a real distinctly-id'd editable clone produced via the existing Duplicate action carrying real Layout/Frame/Representation content over, and a Personal-no-backup card cloning directly into an editable Workspace. Full regression + goldenBuild.js 30/30 unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
